requirement: 'spine status' command only has one item in list with thread id of default. Fix any thread id generation and storage issues. Make sure the 'spine status' command is using real data.

feature_slices.description: Implement UUID-based thread ID generation and a robust state storage mechanism to replace hardcoded 'default' strings. Define the thread model schema, storage interface, and ensure unique ID generation using Python's uuid module. This slice establishes the foundation for accurate thread tracking.
acceptance: Thread IDs are generated using UUID4 or equivalent unique identifier scheme.
Storage layer supports atomic write/read of thread state.
No hardcoded 'default' strings in ID generation or storage.
Schema validates that thread ID is present and unique.

feature_slices.description: Refactor the 'spine status' command to query the actual thread storage, iterate over all stored threads, and display real data. Ensure the command handles empty states gracefully, filters invalid entries, and renders the full list of threads with their correct unique IDs.
acceptance: Command outputs a list containing all stored threads, not just a single item.
Displayed thread IDs match the unique IDs generated and stored.
'default' placeholder no longer appears in output.
Command handles storage errors or empty state with clear user feedback.
Real data is fetched from storage; no mocked or hardcoded test data used.

feature_slices.description: Update thread creation logic to use the new ID generator and persist new threads correctly to storage. Ensure that when a new thread is created, it is assigned a valid UUID and immediately available for retrieval by the status command.
acceptance: New threads are assigned unique UUIDs upon creation.
Created threads are persisted to the storage layer with correct structure.
Status command reflects newly created threads immediately after creation.
Existing thread creation entry points are updated to use the new interface.

feature_slices.description: Implement logic to handle existing stale data, such as threads with 'default' IDs. Provide a migration or cleanup routine that either converts legacy 'default' IDs to valid UUIDs or flags them for removal, ensuring the status command does not display corrupted legacy entries.
acceptance: Existing 'default' thread IDs are detected during load or migration.
Legacy entries are migrated to valid UUIDs or safely removed.
Migration process preserves thread state data where possible.
System runs cleanly with mixed legacy/modern data and produces consistent results.
